# sentry_cron_monitor (Resource)

⚠️ This resource is currently in beta and may be subject to change. It is supported by [New Monitors and Alerts](https://docs.sentry.io/product/new-monitors-and-alerts/) and may not be viewable in the UI today.

Create a Cron Monitor for a Project.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Cron monitor with crontab schedule
resource "sentry_cron_monitor" "crontab" {
  organization = data.sentry_organization.default.slug
  project      = sentry_project.default.slug

  name = "My cron monitor"

  default_assignee = {
    team_id = sentry_team.default.internal_id
  }

  checkin_margin          = 1
  failure_issue_threshold = 1
  max_runtime             = 30
  recovery_threshold      = 30

  schedule = {
    crontab = "0 0 * * *"
  }

  timezone = "UTC"
}

# Cron monitor with interval schedule
resource "sentry_cron_monitor" "crontab" {
  organization = data.sentry_organization.default.slug
  project      = sentry_project.default.slug

  name = "My cron monitor"

  default_assignee = {
    team_id = sentry_team.default.internal_id
  }

  checkin_margin          = 1
  failure_issue_threshold = 1
  max_runtime             = 30
  recovery_threshold      = 30

  schedule = {
    interval_value = 1
    interval_unit  = "day"
  }

  timezone = "UTC"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `checkin_margin` (Number) Grace period. The number of minutes before a check-in is considered missed.
- `failure_issue_threshold` (Number) Failure tolerance. Create a new issue when this many consecutive missed or error check-ins are processed.
- `max_runtime` (Number) Maximum runtime. The number of minutes before an in-progress check-in is marked timed out.
- `name` (String) The name of this monitor.
- `organization` (String) The organization slug or internal ID to create the monitor for.
- `project` (String) The project slug or internal ID to create the monitor for.
- `recovery_threshold` (Number) Recovery Tolerance. Resolve the issue when this many consecutive healthy check-ins are processed. Either `crontab` or `interval_value` and `interval_unit` must be provided.
- `schedule` (Attributes) Set your schedule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--schedule))

### Optional

- `default_assignee` (Attributes) Sentry will assign new issues to this assignee. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--default_assignee))
- `description` (String) A description of the monitor. Will be used in the resulting issue.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the monitor is enabled. Defaults to true.
- `timezone` (String) Timezone. Valid values are: `Africa/Abidjan`, `Africa/Accra`, `Africa/Addis_Ababa`, `Africa/Algiers`, `Africa/Asmara`, `Africa/Asmera`, `Africa/Bamako`, `Africa/Bangui`, `Africa/Banjul`, `Africa/Bissau`, ### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The internal ID of this monitor.

<a id="nestedatt--schedule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule`

Optional:

- `crontab` (String) Use the crontab syntax (e.g. `0 0 * * *`). Conflicts with `interval_value` and `interval_unit`.
- `interval_unit` (String) Interval unit. Conflicts with `crontab`. Must be provided with `interval_value`. Valid values are: `year`, `month`, `week`, `day`, `hour`, and `minute`.
- `interval_value` (Number) Interval value. Conflicts with `crontab`. Must be provided with `interval_unit`.


<a id="nestedatt--default_assignee"></a>
### Nested Schema for `default_assignee`

Optional:

- `team_id` (String) The team internal ID to assign new issues to. Conflicts with `user_id`.
- `user_id` (String) The user ID to assign new issues to. Conflicts with `team_id`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# import using the organization and team slugs from the URL:
# https://[org-slug].sentry.io/monitors/[monitor-id]/
terraform import sentry_cron_monitor.default org-slug/project-slug/monitor-id
```
